sourceMovie.title: Salò, or the 120 Days of Sodom
sourceMovie.whyPeopleLoveIt: Audiences latched onto Salò, or the 120 Days of Sodom (1976) as a drama theatrical experience: big enough for a group watch, specific enough to reward a second viewing once you know where it's headed. The drama and horror blend is the engine: it lets the film shift registers without feeling like two different movies stitched together.

recommendations.title: The Girl Next Door
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: If Salò, or the 120 Days of Sodom clicked for you, The Girl Next Door (2007) is a strong next stop: both trade in drama and horror, with unease that lingers after the credits. Expect a different story, but a similar willingness to go big when the moment demands it.

faq.question: What should I watch after Salò, or the 120 Days of Sodom?
faq.answer: Try The Girl Next Door, An American Crime, The Devils—each shares DNA with Salò, or the 120 Days of Sodom while changing the setting enough to feel fresh.

faq.question: What genre is Salò, or the 120 Days of Sodom?
faq.answer: Salò, or the 120 Days of Sodom (1976) is best described as Drama, Horror. Stores and algorithms use different labels, but that mix is what fans actually recognize on a rewatch.

faq.question: Is Salò, or the 120 Days of Sodom worth revisiting?
faq.answer: Viewer scores sit around 6.3/10 on TMDB—high enough that replays are common for people who like this drama lane.
